LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS SAM PATEL AND 599 POST STREET, LLC'S SPECIAL DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT AND MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF- THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1 AND 2, TO WHICH DEFENDANT DEMURRED ON GROUNDS OF UNCERTAINTY BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLEADED THAT DEFENDANT INCREASED RENT BEYOND THAT ALLOWED BY SECTION 37.11 OR A FAILURE TO ACCOMMODATE. AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 6 THE COURT CONSTRUES THE DEMURRER AS OBJECTING TO PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH C.R.C. 2.112(2) IN THAT PLAINTIFF HAS COMBINED INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND BATTERY IN ONE CAUSE OF ACTION. AND THE COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER TO THE BATTERY CAUSE OF ACTION WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO SEPARATELY STATE SUCH A CLAIM, IF PLAINTIFF CAN ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH AN UNLAWFUL TOUCHING. THE DEMURRER IS OTHERWISE OVERRULED. THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED AS TO THE PRAYER FOR TREBLE DAMAGES BUT OTHERWISE DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189 (301/PJB)
CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT PATEL, SAM AN INDIVIDUAL 599 POST STREET,LLC A CORPORATION JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 5.0 DAYS

OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO STRKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, AND REQUEST FOR CONTEMPT CITATION UNDER CCP 1008 AND SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2000 FILED BY PLAINTIFF FELIX, JOSEPH H.

Defendant Sam Patel'S Demurrer
Off Calendar Jun-11-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANTS SAM PATEL AND 599 POST STREET, LLC'S SPECIAL DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT AND MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF- THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1 AND 2, TO WHICH DEFENDANT DEMURRED ON GROUNDS OF UNCERTAINTY BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLEADED THAT DEFENDANT INCREASED RENT BEYOND THAT ALLOWED BY SECTION 37.11 OR A FAILURE TO ACCOMMODATE. AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 6 THE COURT CONSTRUES THE DEMURRER AS OBJECTING TO PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH C.R.C. 2.112(2) IN THAT PLAINTIFF HAS COMBINED INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND BATTERY IN ONE CAUSE OF ACTION. AND THE COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER TO THE BATTERY CAUSE OF ACTION WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO SEPARATELY STATE SUCH A CLAIM, IF PLAINTIFF CAN ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH AN UNLAWFUL TOUCHING. THE DEMURRER IS OTHERWISE OVERRULED.
THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED AS TO THE PRAYER FOR TREBLE DAMAGES BUT OTHERWISE DENIED. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189 =(301/PJB/AL)
